notes: Valid TEMP frames arrive every 100 ms while the controller can still be stepped every 50 ms.
The chamber stores residual heat, so temperature can keep rising briefly after HEATER OFF.
A controller that waits too long to switch off can still overshoot after the actuator changes.

task.objective: Warm the chamber into the working band, contain thermal lag near the upper edge, and fail safe to heater-off on bad input.
interface: Controller inputs are CONTROLLER_INPUT_TEMPERATURE, CONTROLLER_INPUT_SENSOR_TIMEOUT, CONTROLLER_INPUT_INVALID_TEMPERATURE, and CONTROLLER_INPUT_NONE.
Controller output exposes heater_on, state, state_name, changed, and timed_out.
Sensor frames use `SENSE TEMP <integer>` and actuator frames must be `ACT HEATER ON` or `ACT HEATER OFF`.
policy: On boot, keep the heater off.
If 47 <= TEMP < 50, hold the previous safe actuator state.
If 50 <= TEMP <= 52 and TEMP is rising compared with the previous valid TEMP sample, command HEATER OFF early to absorb thermal lag.
If 50 <= TEMP <= 52 and TEMP is not rising, hold the previous safe actuator state.
On malformed sensor input, command HEATER OFF.
On explicit sensor timeout, command HEATER OFF.
If no valid sensor frame arrives for more than 1000 ms, command HEATER OFF.
decision_summary: 47 <= TEMP < 50: preserve the previous safe output, state HOLDING
50 <= TEMP <= 52 and rising: heater_on = false, state HOLDING
TIMEOUT or INVALID input: heater_on = false, state SAFE_OFF
state_model: SAFE_OFF: fail-safe off or above-band off
HEATING: actively driving the heater on because temperature is low
HOLDING: inside the working band while preserving the previous safe actuator state

constraints: Keep the existing public controller interface intact.
Heater commands must always be ACT HEATER ON or ACT HEATER OFF.
Temperature values are expected to stay within 15..80.
Any malformed or stale sensor input must drive the controller to the safe OFF state.
Internal helper names and code organization are not graded; only the required public behavior and runtime behavior are.

notes: The hidden plant includes thermal lag, so a late HEATER OFF can still overshoot after the actuator changes.
The overshoot scenarios are intended to require a small amount of state across valid sensor samples.
